title: Advice on Cholesterol levels please
content:
Hi there , can someone give me some advice please?I just got my bloods done on an annual check. The GP wants to increase my statins, simvastatin 10mg to 20mg a day.
I am not keen, as they give me side effects, but I shall take them if I have to.
I never got to speak to the GP, the results were just relayed through the receptionist. I asked for a copy via email.
I could do better myself to try and reduce my level and this is something that I need to take on board. I am female 64 and a bit overweight.
summary: Hi there , can someone give me some advice please?I just got my bloods done on an annual check. The GP wants to increase my statins, simvastatin 10mg to 20mg a day.
author: oscar2

title: Linda Lincolnshire
content: When I started the menopause my cholesterol went up to 10 even on a controlled diet. This was thirty five years ago. It took a year to eventually control my cholesterol on 80 mg simvastatin. When my Doctor retired my new Doctor said it could be controlled on 40mg Atorvastatin. I agreed and it did control my cholesterol. Five years later I asked for a cholesterol test to see how things were. The Doctor agreed and when the results came on my NHS app I got a real shock. Yes my cholesterol was fine but my triglycerides were over twice the normal level. Luckily I was due a medicine review and the Pharmacist said I had to start upping my statins. I am now back on 80mg Atorvastatin. I have been told I have a combined lipid problem. I am now aged seventy six and I remember my first Doctor all those years ago said he could not understand why I needed such a high dose of statins. My advice is be careful and download the NHS app and check all your results. I am lucky I can tolerate such a high dose of statins.
summary: When I started the menopause my cholesterol went up to 10 even on a controlled diet. This was thirty five years ago. It took a year to eventually control my cholesterol on 80 mg simvastatin. When my Doctor retired my new Doctor said it could be controlled on 40mg Atorvastatin. I agreed and it did control my cholesterol. Five years later I asked for a cholesterol test to see how things were. The Doctor agreed and when the results came on my NHS app I got a real shock. Yes my cholesterol was fine but my triglycerides were over twice the normal level. Luckily I was due a medicine review and the Pharmacist said I had to start upping my statins. I am now back on 80mg Atorvastatin. I have been told I have a combined lipid problem. I am now aged seventy six and I remember my first Doctor all those years ago said he could not understand why I needed such a high dose of statins. My advice is be careful and download the NHS app and check all your results. I am lucky I can tolerate such a high dose of statins.
author: Linda_Lincolnshire62
